mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes that allows you to back up your cluster resources and persistent volumes, facilitating disaster recovery and migration. It is particularly useful for managing backups in production environments and can be integrated with various cloud storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential complexity in managing backup schedules and storage locations.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This repository contains the Velero Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Velero with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Velero in the 'velero' namespace with the specified version and configuration.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION>

```

> Replace <YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> and <YOUR_AWS_REGION> with your actual AWS S3 bucket name and region.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION>

steps.title: Verify Velero installation
steps.description:
Check that the Velero server is running and the necessary resources are created.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> You should see the Velero pod in a Running state.

steps.title: Create a backup
steps.description:
This command creates a backup of your Kubernetes resources and persistent volumes.

```bash

velero backup create my-backup --include-namespaces <YOUR_NAMESPACE>

```

> Replace <YOUR_NAMESPACE> with the namespace you want to back up.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This command removes Velero and all associated resources. Be cautious as this will delete all backups stored in the configured storage location.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This will remove the Velero release from your cluster.
> This will delete the Velero namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current Velero configuration. This process includes draining any workloads if necessary, upgrading Velero, and verifying the installation.

```bash

velero backup create pre-upgrade-backup --include-namespaces <YOUR_NAMESPACE>

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> Create a backup before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the Velero pod is running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup: unable to create backup storage location'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error typically occurs when the specified S3 bucket does not exist or the credentials are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify that the S3 bucket exists and that the credentials used by Velero have the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Backup failed: unable to list resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Velero service account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Velero service account has the correct RBAC permissions to list resources in the specified namespaces.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Volume snapshot location not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified volume snapshot location is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the Velero configuration and ensure that the volume snapshot location is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Backup already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when trying to create a backup with the same name as an existing backup.

**Fix:**
Use a different name for the backup or delete the existing backup before creating a new one.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for additional cloud providers, improved backup performance, and enhanced logging features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of certain legacy flags in backup commands.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17, ensure that your backup storage locations are updated to the new configuration format.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero pod running with a status of 'Running' and the output of 'kubectl get pods -n velero' should list the Velero pod without errors.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION>

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
